ANGELES, November 2017 /3BL Media/ celebration National Philanthropy Day, AEG, world's leading sports live entertainment company, announced plans annual Season Giving, company's initiative that encourages employees throughout world engage charitable activities their local communities HOPE.
ACT.
GIVE.
During initiative throughout November December, focuses philanthropic efforts raise funds generate awareness nonprofit organizations working substantive issues related hunger, homelessness, healthcare education.
Throughout Season Giving, encourages employees participate multiple service days throughout their communities, collectively spending thousands hours volunteering making positive impact local schools, homeless shelters community organizations.
Southern California, participating venues AEG-owned companies include STAPLES Center, StubHub Center, Microsoft Theater, L.A. LIVE, Shrine Auditorium, Fonda Theater, Novo Microsoft, Kings Galaxy among other divisions.
"AEG's Season Giving only spotlights efforts nonprofit organizations brings together employees community partners work side-by-side with collective goal improving their respective communities," said Martha Saucedo, Executive Vice President, External Affairs, AEG.

Throughout past years, more than 85,000 walkers have raised more than $7.6 million support homeless population Angeles.

homeless shelter offers emergency services, 12-step recovery program, training, education work programs that lead productive lives.
